search_queries_used: site:resus.org.uk anaphylaxis 2021 recognition trigger follow-up official
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Medicine names, doses, routes, repeat timing and treatment sequence were excluded.
Diagnosis, observation duration and discharge remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with clinician-suspected anaphylaxis; pregnancy, infants, comorbidity and refractory reactions require qualified review.
setting_applicability: Emergency recognition and documentation; prehospital and post-event pathways require local adaptation.
UAE_applicability: Recognition principles transfer, but UAE emergency response, medicine, ambulance, observation, referral and discharge policies apply.
recency_verification: The RCUK May 2021 exact guideline was reviewed on 2026-07-14; no newer replacement was identified.
superseded_check: The reviewed RCUK 2021 guideline remains the current registered source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCUK recognition section supports documenting rapid onset, severity, associated features, relevant negatives, vital or appearance findings and time-critical airway, breathing or circulation abnormalities only when clinician-assessed; it does not auto-diagnose anaphylaxis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact trigger and follow-up section supports recording specialist discussion or arranged follow-up only when completed by the clinician.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact trigger, exposure route/time, skin and mucosal findings, airway/breathing/circulation details, treatment, medicine, dose, response, observation, discharge and patient instructions remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
